title: MVC
objectives: Know how MVC differs from MVP and MVVM
Understand why MVVM/MVI dominate modern Flutter

sections.markdown: MVC (Model-View-Controller) is a classic UI pattern where the **Controller** receives input, updates the **Model**, and the **View** renders the Model. Common on the server (Spring MVC) and iOS UIKit, less directly used in modern Flutter.

In classic web MVC (Rails/Spring), the Controller handles a request, calls the Model, and returns a View. In iOS UIKit's MVC the Controller became a 'Massive View Controller' anti-pattern — one big class doing everything.
For Flutter, prefer MVVM (or MVI/BLoC). MVC is worth knowing because backend frameworks and legacy iOS code use it, and interviewers compare the three.

flashcards.back: Model (data/rules), View (renders), Controller (handles input, updates model).
MVVM's observable state fits Flutter's declarative UI better than direct controller updates.
An iOS anti-pattern where all logic lives in one huge UIViewController.

options: Handles input and updates the Model

quiz.explain: Spring MVC, Rails, UIKit — that's classic MVC's home.
Observable-state patterns match Flutter's declarative UI.

challenges.text: Diagram MVC vs MVVM for a login screen.
Take a classic Spring MVC endpoint and map its parts onto Flutter.
Convert a Massive-View-Controller-style Dart file into MVVM.
Compare testability of the same feature under MVC vs MVVM with unit tests.

interviewQuestions: Why is MVC less natural in Flutter?
What is the Massive View Controller anti-pattern?
How does MVC show up on the server side?
When would you choose MVC over MVVM?
